# Microsoft Customer Engagement Methodology (MCEM) — Stage Reference

> **Tier 3 reference document.** Never auto-loaded. Read by agent when detailed MCEM stage definitions, exit criteria, or role accountability details are needed.

## MCEM Overview

MCEM is a unified engagement model connecting sales, specialists, customer success, services, and partners across five stages:

1. Listen & Consult
2. Inspire & Design
3. Empower & Achieve
4. Realize Value
5. Manage & Optimize

Accountability shifts across stages:

- **ATU** leads early customer understanding and qualification
- **STU** leads solution shaping and deal commitment
- **CSU** leads post-sale value realization and expansion

## Unit → Agent Role Mapping

| MCEM Unit | Full Name | Agent Roles |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ATU | Account Team Unit | Account Executive (out of scope for agent skills) |
| STU | Specialist Team Unit | **Specialist**, **Solution Engineer (SE)** |
| CSU | Customer Success Unit | **CSAM**, **Cloud Solution Architect (CSA)** |
| Partners | — | Referenced contextually |

---

## Stage 1 — Listen & Consult

**Primary Objective**: Understand customer needs, desired outcomes, and qualify whether an opportunity exists.

**Core Operations**:

- Consume signals, leads, and insights
- Initial customer outreach and discovery
- Stakeholder mapping and problem definition
- Opportunity qualification
- Confirm initial Solution Play alignment
- Engage partners and support roles as needed

**Role Accountability**: ATU — accountable | Partners — engaged for early planning | Support/Services — consulted

**Exit Criteria**:

- Qualified opportunity
- Customer needs and outcomes clearly defined
- Initial Solution Play selected

---

## Stage 2 — Inspire & Design

**Primary Objective**: Shape the solution vision and align customer stakeholders on value and approach.

**Core Operations**:

- Orchestrate Microsoft and partner team
- Conduct envisioning workshops
- Capture customer value (e.g., Business Value Assessment)
- Define technical and business proof requirements
- Create or update Customer Success Plan
- Secure programs, funding, or investments

**Role Accountability**: STU — accountable | ATU — co-orchestrates | Partners — solution alignment and co-sell

**Exit Criteria**:

- Solution Plays confirmed
- Business value reviewed and endorsed by customer
- Customer Success Plan created

---

## Stage 3 — Empower & Achieve

**Primary Objective**: Prove feasibility, finalize the deal, and secure customer commitment.

**Core Operations**:

- Deliver technical and/or business proof (POC, MVP, Pilot, Demo)
- Finalize architecture and solution design
- Evaluate deal strategy and capacity
- Create and present proposal
- Engage legal, procurement, and negotiation
- Secure customer agreement

**Role Accountability**: STU — accountable | Deal Desk / Legal / Finance — engaged | Partners — delivery and co-sell execution

**Exit Criteria**:

- Customer agreement in place
- Resources aligned to delivery plan
- Outcomes committed and baseline metrics defined

---

## Stage 4 — Realize Value

**Primary Objective**: Deliver the solution and ensure customer outcomes are achieved.

**Core Operations**:

- Deliver solution against agreed outcomes
- Initiate customer training and change management
- Monitor usage and adoption
- Track business value realization
- Update Customer Success Plan

**Role Accountability**: CSU — accountable | Services / Partners — delivery and adoption

**Exit Criteria**:

- Solution delivered successfully
- Customer health metrics agreed
- Business value tracking in place

---

## Stage 5 — Manage & Optimize

**Primary Objective**: Sustain value, drive consumption, and identify expansion opportunities.

**Core Operations**:

- Monitor usage trends and health signals
- Proactive backlog and success plan reviews
- Identify expansion or renewal signals
- Refresh consumption and success plans
- Ensure customer can operate and maintain solution independently

**Role Accountability**: CSU — accountable | ATU / STU — re-engaged for expansion

**Exit Criteria**:

- Outcomes met and sustained
- Next customer needs identified
- Opportunity completed with next steps defined

---

## Non-Linear Mechanics

- MCEM stages may iterate or overlap
- Different Seller Journeys alter role sequencing and timing
- Qualified pipeline typically begins at Stage 2
- Billed opportunities are often won at the end of Stage 3
- Verifiable Outcomes and Exit Criteria are captured in MSX/D365
- Partner involvement varies by segment and motion
